title: pedicularis axillaris subsp. balfouriana
intro: Pedicularis axillaris subsp. balfouriana, also known as Balfour's lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the balm family, Pedicularis, which is native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Pedicularis axillaris subsp. balfouriana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
